ID:       ddx-256af8b5
Title:    S15-7: Multi-node trust attestation forwarding (Story-14 coupling)
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:15, kind:multi-node, blocking, triage:needs-investigation
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-1d85c927
Deps:     ddx-a7e7a1a1
Desc:     PROBLEM
BLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement. In the coordinator topology (Story 14), the originating peer identity attestation is not forwarded from coordinator to client — any non-localhost write goes through the hub without carrying who initiated it. The spoke cannot authorize on both origin and coordinator identity because neither is transmitted.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: current federation supports query fan-out only. No RouteMutationToProjectOwner, no ForwardMutation, no identity header propagation.
- Risk ('Multi-node authorization gap'): A compromised or misconfigured hub can forward mutations on behalf of any origin, with the spoke unable to distinguish legitimate coordinator-forwarded requests from hub-originated attacks. Without dual authorization, spoke trusts the hub implicitly.
- The /tmp/story-15-final.md plan file cited in the original bead is ephemeral and not available to sub-agents on other machines; relevant content is inlined below.

PLAN-LOCKED DECISIONS (from story-15 planning):
- Multi-node routing (point 6): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations.
- Identity forwarding: X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified WhoIs peer at coordinator edge (NOT coordinator self); X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Both mandatory on forwarded mutations.
- Dual authorization on spoke: requireTrusted augmented to check BOTH origin (WhoIs / loopback policy) AND coordinator (registered federation hub); 403 if either fails.

This bead has been decomposed into 4 children:
- ddx-468d800f — S15-7a: ADR-conformance lint for mutation resolvers.
- ddx-eb75a32d — S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation).
- ddx-ba31fec8 — S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization.
- ddx-ea1ada47 — S15-7d: Integration test with two in-process servers.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT spec; changes go into FEAT-010 and FEAT-011 extensions.
- Performance optimization of federation routing.
- Story 14 query fan-out behavior (already shipped in fanout.go).
Accept:   1. Coordinator forwards origin identity (X-DDx-Origin-Identity) unchanged in mutation envelope from ddx-eb75a32d.
2. Client-side authorization (ddx-ba31fec8) checks both origin and coordinator identity; returns 403 when either missing or untrusted.
3. ADR-conformance lint (ddx-468d800f) added to CI and fails on any new mutation resolver missing requireTrusted or CSRF/Origin checks.
4. TestRequireTrusted_ForwardedMutation_AuthorizesOriginAndCoordinator passes (happy path).
5. TestFederation_OperatorPromptSubmit_CoordinatorToSpoke_IdentityUnchanged (ddx-ea1ada47) exercises full stack.
6. Three negative sub-tests (untrusted-coordinator, missing-origin, tampered-origin) all return 403.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
